skill.name: concept-scaffolding
skill.description: Design progressive learning scaffolds that manage cognitive load, prerequisites, worked examples, and validation checkpoints for complex concepts.
skill.summary: Build tiered lesson scaffolds that move learners from foundation to independent application.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives in markdown prose and Python teaching examples. The skill contains instructional guidance for lesson scaffolding and no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, destructive behavior, or prompt-injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick and fenced-code locations are instructional markdown and Python examples for teaching decorators. They are not executable skill scripts and do not invoke a shell or external process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are inside prose, markdown fences, and Python teaching snippets. No shell invocation, command runner, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in Educational Prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on multiple prose lines, but review found no cryptographic implementation, hashing API, password handling, or security-sensitive algorithm choice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations describe cognitive scaffolding, checkpoints, or layer concepts. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or any cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern in Benign Planning Text
low_findings.description: The scanner reported system reconnaissance at prose lines that discuss learner checks and instructional planning. No operating system discovery, environment probing, or host inventory behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching text is natural-language educational guidance. There is no command, API call, or instruction to inspect a machine or collect system details.

content.value_statement: Complex concepts often overwhelm learners when prerequisites, examples, and checkpoints are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create staged learning plans with cognitive load controls.
actual_capabilities: Diagnoses why a concept is difficult for a target learner level.
Maps prerequisites before dependent ideas are introduced.
Builds three to seven progressive steps based on cognitive load.
Adds worked examples before learner practice tasks.
Defines validation checkpoints for each learning step.
Aligns AI assistance with learner proficiency and task complexity.
limitations: Does not verify learning outcomes with real learner data.
Depends on accurate input about learner level and prerequisites.
Does not generate full course assets without additional prompting.
Provides instructional structure, not domain fact checking.

use_cases.title: Plan a Technical Lesson
use_cases.description: Break a programming topic into prerequisite checks, worked examples, practice, and validation checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Design Onboarding Modules
use_cases.description: Convert complex product or process knowledge into staged training that avoids overwhelming new team members.

use_cases.title: Improve Self-Study Prompts
use_cases.description: Create structured prompts that move a learner from basic understanding to independent application.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Scaffold
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the learner is new to the concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold this concept for a beginner: [concept]. Include prerequisites, three to five steps, one worked example per step, and a checkpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Lesson Redesign
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when existing material feels too dense.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this lesson outline and redesign it with cognitive load limits, dependency ordering, worked examples, and validation checks: [outline].

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate Technical Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for learners who know the basics but need guided application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a B1-level scaffolding plan for [technical topic]. Show the complexity diagnosis, learner state, step sequence, AI support points, and failure checks.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Curriculum Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for multi-stage learning paths or expert review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a full scaffolding architecture for [domain]. Map prerequisites, concept density, learner tiers, transfer tasks, validation strategy, and adjustment rules.

output_examples.input: Scaffold Python decorators for intermediate learners.
output_examples.output: A five-step plan that starts with functions as objects, moves through closures and wrappers, introduces decorator syntax, and ends with optional decorator factories. Each step includes load level, support type, and a checkpoint.

output_examples.input: Make our onboarding material easier for new support agents.
output_examples.output: A staged onboarding scaffold with prerequisite vocabulary, simple ticket examples, realistic customer scenarios, escalation practice, and checks before independent work.

output_examples.input: Review whether my lesson jumps too quickly into advanced ideas.
output: Identifies missing prerequisites and overloaded steps.
Reorders concepts from foundation to application.
Adds worked examples and micro-checks before independent tasks.

best_practices: Provide the learner level, existing knowledge, and target outcome before asking for a scaffold.
Ask for cognitive load notes and validation checkpoints for every step.
Use the output as a draft and test it with real learner feedback.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a generic outline without learner context.
Do not skip prerequisite mapping for complex technical concepts.
Do not treat the step count as fixed before diagnosing concept difficulty.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured learning scaffolds with prerequisites, progressive steps, worked examples, and checkpoints.

faq.question: Is this only for programming topics?
faq.answer: No. It can support any complex concept, but the included example focuses on Python decorators.

faq.question: Does it write complete lessons?
faq.answer: It designs the instructional structure. You can ask for full lesson text after the scaffold is approved.

faq.question: How does it manage cognitive load?
faq.answer: It limits new concepts per step, orders dependencies, and adds checks before increasing complexity.

faq.question: Can it adapt to different learner levels?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for proficiency level and adjusts concept density, scaffolding strength, and AI assistance.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only markdown instructional content and false-positive static findings.
